license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.rtzd6p6
title: High-throughput assessment of changes in the Caenorhabditis elegans gut microbiome
author: Fan Zhang

steps_raw.instruction: Wash worms from bacterial lawns with M9 buffer + 0.01 % volume Triton X to a sterilized 2 ml 96-well deep plate. Use 1000 µL M9+ Triton X to wash worms from a 12-well plate Use 600 µL M9+ Triton X to wash worms from a 24-well plate M9 Medium by Adrien Assie
Bring each well to 1.8 mL total with M9 + 0.01 % volume Triton X using a multichannel pipette

Centrifuge deep well plate at 00:01:00, at 300g to pellet down worms, then remove liquid by using an aspirating manifold. Alternatively worms can be allowed to settle in the deep well plate by gravity without centrifugation to separate adults from progeny. (V&P scientific, VP1171A, Aspirating manifold, allow liquid aspiration from 96 well plates)
Repeat steps 2-3 5 times with M9 to further remove bacteria in liquid.
Bring the volume in each well down to 100 µL using the aspirating manifold.

Add 100 µL of 10 millimolar (mM) levamisole in 1X M9 + 0.01% Triton X to each well and allow the worms to paralyze for 00:05:00 . Confirm paralysis via microscopy before proceeding to the next step. Levamisole Acros Organics Catalog #16595-80-5
Add 200 µL of 4% bleach solution (2:1 Bleach:5M NaOH) in M9 to each well for 00:02:00 This will further eliminate bacteria in liquid and attached on worm cuticle.
Repeat step 2-3 2 times with 1X M9 + 0.01 % volume Triton X to remove bleach and levamisole.
Save 100 µL of supernatant from the last wash to use as a control to assess the background bacterial residual before host lysis.
Bring liquid volume in each well to 1 mL 1X M9 + 0.01 % volume Triton X, mix and transfer 100 µL of the mixture to a flat bottom 96 well plate (Costar 3370, Corning).
Take bright field images for each well for later analysis of worm population size, life stages, and individual length.
Aspirate remaining liquid volume down to 300 µL and add 1.0 mm sterilized garnet beads (Biospect) to each well (enough to cover well bottom).
Seal the plate with a sterile plastic mat and lyse worms in Mixer Mill (Restch) or TissueLyser at 25 Hz speed for 5 min. 00:05:00, Bead beating
Mix by pipetting and transfer 20 µL of cell lysate to a 96-well plate prepared with 180 µL 1x PBS per well (1:10 dilution) and mix
Transfer 20 µL of the dilution from step 14 to 180 µL LB in 96 well flat bottom for liquid OD growth assay.
Centrifuge the deep-well plate at 4000g for 10 mins to pellet worms and bacteria, then bring the remaining liquid volume down to 190 µL 00:10:00, Centrifugation at 4000g
Freeze the lysate in -80 °C freezer overnight.DNA extraction and 16S rRNA PCR Undetermined, Freezing

Thaw the plate and add 0.1 mm sterile zirconia/silica beads (BioSpec products) to each well (enough to cover well bottom) and bead-beat in Mixer Mill (Restch) or TissueLyser for 5 mins to further lyse bacterial cells. 00:05:00, Shaking/Lysis

Fill a flat-bottom 96-well plate with 180 µL of sterile filtered PBS. Pipette 20 µL of the microbiome mixture used in the assay into four replicate wells in column 1 of the plate (e.g., A1, B1, C1, and D1). For controls, pipet 20 µL of LB in the remaining wells of column 1.
Using a multichannel pipette, serially dilute the microbiome mixture (1:10) in columns 2-12 to create a dilution series for all wells of the plate. Change tips between each dilution/column.

From the serial dilution plate, plate 10 µL of each dilution onto a rectangular LB plate using a multichannel pipette. Incubate at optimal growth temperature overnight. Count colonies for each of the wells where possible.

Prepare a growth plate by filling each well of a flat bottom 96-well plate with 180 µL of LB. Transfer 20 μL from the serial dilution plate to the corresponding well of the growth plate. Incubate at the optimal temperature with intermittent shaking and monitor OD 600 regularly (e.g., every 15-30 min) until growth is observed in all wells.

Use a plotting software (Microsoft Excel, R or otherwise), to visualize growth curves by plotting OD 600 reading vs time for each well on a single chart. Verify that the growth patterns are consistent between replicates and growth curves are ordered by the dilution factor.

Identify the range of OD 600 values within the linear portion of the growth curves. Use the linear range identified and the following equation to calculate the optical density Threshold: Threshold = \frac{(OD_{600max} - OD_{600min})}{2} + OD_{600min}
Using the only data points where the OD 600 readings for each well fall between the linear portion, calculate the Slope and Intercept for each well
To calculate the time at which each well reached the threshold (Y-axis of calibration curve), use the following equation for each well: Time = \frac{(Threshold - Intercept)}{Slope}

Plot CFU values collected in Step 19 vs.Time of Threshold crossing for each well, and calculate the R squared value and exponential trendline equation. Use the obtained equation to calculate CFU from the respective threshold time of each sample.
Determine colonization levels on a per animal basis for each sample using the following formula: CFU / animal = \frac{(CFU * Dilution factor)}{number of animals}
Add 10 µL of 20 mg/ml proteinase K to each well.
In a ThermoCycler run a program as follow: 01:00:00, step at 60 °C 00:15:00, step at 95 °C to desactivate the proteinase K keep on hold at 4 °C

Centrifuge the plate 00:10:00, at 4000g to pellet down cellular fractions. Transfer 100 µL of supernatant to a clean 96 well PCR plate. If using the plate again soon, place at 4 °C , otherwise, store at -80 °C Use filter tips for this step, and do not allow the area above the ‘groove’ of the tips to enter the plate
In a 5 mL microcentrifuge tube, mix: 1.25 mL of 2x Taq Master Mix (1 tube) 100 µL of 515F primer 1 mL H 2 O
Add 23 µL of newly mixed master mix into each well of a new 96 well PCR plate
Add 1 µL of reverse primer 806R to each well of the 96 well PCR plate.
Add 1 µL of the supernatant from the PCR plate prepared in step 23 as DNA template for the PCR. The remaining supernatant can be frozen at -80 °C for storage.
Place the PCR plate in the ThermoCycler and run the following program %s %s
Verify PCR amplification results with an agarose gel. Prepare a 1.5% agarose gel. Mix 2 µL of loading dye with 4 µL of PCR product per sample and load 5 µL in the gel along with 1 kb ladder.
Purify PCR product using commercial PCR purification kit.

Quantify the intensity of PCR product band from each library (gel or fluorometry), normalize PCR product amount by volume and pool libraries together with an equivalent amount of PCR product into a single tube.
Submit samples for multiplex sequencing along with sequencing primer
